scenarios.opening_query: My sourdough course videos are being resold on a course platform by someone claiming they're his.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: The immediate machinery is the platform's — DMCA takedowns, repeat-infringer policy, and a subpoena for the anonymous reseller's identity; platform procedure precedes any suit.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The immediate machinery is the platform's — DMCA takedowns and a subpoena to unmask the reseller come before any suit.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether platform/unmasking procedure is the first phase
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I know exactly who he is — a former student, local, with assets; the registered-copyright infringement suit is ready now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I know who he is — a local former student with assets. The infringement suit is ready.

scenarios.opening_query: Our farm's heirloom apple name is suddenly on a big grocery chain's house-brand cider.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Business and Corporate > Agriculture/Farm
scenarios.hidden_fact: The name is a plant-variety denomination we hold through our nursery licensing program — the enforcement runs through plant-variety and ag-licensing law, which is its own world.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The name is our plant-variety denomination under our nursery licensing — plant-variety and ag-licensing law is the enforcement world.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether plant-variety/ag licensing governs the name
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No variety rights — it's our registered farm brand on their label, a straight trademark case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No variety rights — it's our registered brand on their label.

scenarios.opening_query: I built our restaurant's entire online ordering system and the owner just fired me and kept it running.
scenarios.initial: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law || Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I built it on the clock as the manager — it's likely theirs, honestly; what they actually owe me is 400 hours of unpaid overtime from those build months, and that's the claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I built it on the clock, so it's likely theirs — what they owe me is the 400 hours of unpaid overtime from those months.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the real claim is unpaid labor rather than ownership
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I built it nights on my own equipment under no agreement — and they've locked me out of my own repo while running my code.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I built it nights on my own gear with no agreement — and they locked me out of my own repo.
scenarios.vetting_note: Work-scope honesty flips ownership into wage claims.

scenarios.opening_query: A toy company's new plush line looks exactly like the characters from my self-published kids' books.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Two years ago I pitched them under an NDA with full character sheets — the NDA breach with documented access is the stronger, cleaner case than any similarity fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I pitched them these characters under NDA two years ago — the NDA breach with documented access is the cleaner case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an NDA with access evidence exists
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Never any contact — pure copying of registered characters; the infringement case stands alone.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No contact ever — pure copying of registered characters.

scenarios.opening_query: The app I commissioned from a dev shop turned up rebranded in another country's app store.
scenarios.initial: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law || International Law > Business And Corporate
scenarios.hidden_fact: The dev shop is in Bangalore and our contract selects Indian law and arbitration — cross-border enforcement through that clause is the actual path.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The shop's in Bangalore and the contract selects Indian law and arbitration — cross-border enforcement is the path.
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The shop's domestic — the foreign listing is just their alias account, and the US case with app-store takedowns proceeds normally.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The shop's domestic — the foreign listing is their alias. US case plus takedowns.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone trademarked the name of the open-source project I've maintained for nine years.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Business and Corporate > Non-Profit
scenarios.hidden_fact: The community's real fix is structural — forming the long-planned nonprofit foundation to hold the marks, take the opposition, and own the name collectively; the entity comes first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The real fix is forming the nonprofit foundation to hold the marks and take the opposition — the entity comes first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether entity formation is the chosen vehicle
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No entity plans — I'll oppose the filing personally with my nine years of first-use evidence, and the deadline is in five weeks.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No entity — I'll oppose personally with nine years of first-use evidence. Five weeks to deadline.
